title: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) Calculator
metaDescription: Calculate your true Customer Acquisition Cost by combining sales and marketing spend, instantly in your browser.
shortDescription: Calculate CAC from total sales and marketing spend divided by new customers acquired.

guideSections.heading: Why Marketing Spend Alone Understates True CAC
guideSections.body: A common mistake is calculating CAC using only ad spend, while ignoring sales team salaries, commissions, and tooling costs that also went into acquiring those customers. A complete CAC figure includes the full sales and marketing expenditure for the period, not just the most visible line item.

guideSections.heading: Why CAC Means Nothing Without LTV Context
guideSections.body: A CAC of $200 is excellent for a customer paying $5,000 a year and disastrous for one paying $15 a month. CAC is a diagnostic number, not a verdict on its own — it needs to be weighed against customer lifetime value (LTV) before judging whether an acquisition channel is actually profitable.
